messages.message: I have no immediate plans to interfere with your positions. If you require support in the future, let me know.

Hi Germany! Would you be interested in cooperating to stop the western juggernaut that so frequently dominates the game? I think that our two nations make natural allies given our central positioning and how our forces can move outward from one another without being a threat to each other!
I intend to consolidate my units in Italy first. If your movements affect me, I may need to respond.

Greetings!
I reckon a juggernaut could be quite effective and easy to disguise early on. Would you be interested in that?

Hey France!

I think we make natural allies and would really like to work together. Any interest in pulling a Western Triple or just a straight up EF? Either way, I hope we can agree that we both benefit a lot by not fighting each other.

Hey Russia!

I hope we can remain friendly. To that end, I'll send a more detailed message later, but my main goal is to find a way to work together.
Hey England! I'm very much the same - a EF is great for me so long as we can do it in such a way that you don't open strongly against me, and I'm happy to help you against Germany in that case!
Excuse the double message :p
Hi Austria! I hope that we can agree to share information that we hear concerning the other. We also make good allies given our locations, being natural allies in the middle of the board. Good luck!

Hey France,
Glad to hear from you! I think this is a great idea and would love to cooperate. How would you like to split the low countries/england?

Greetings!
What do you say about working together against Austria? I see Austria as a potential problem to both of us.
Agreed. Can we agree to DMZ the Channel?
Also I think it's important that we DMZ Burgundy.

Hey Italy! Appreciate that. I think AI makes a ton of sense since it's much easier for us to move in separate directions, but if we get locked up and get our houses in order I would be happy to work together!

Hey England!

I'm hoping we can be allies this game! What are you thinking for the early game? How do you want to handle Belgium and Sweden?
I assume you mean "your movements affect me", since you are Italy. I would be open to a DMZ in Piedmont if that would help.

Greetings!
While I doubt we will meet each other for a long time, just wanted to open diplomatic communications.
Hey France! I agree, I will keep you in the loop. Best of luck this game!

Hey Russia,

I'm hoping we can be allies this game! What are you thinking for the early game? How do you want to handle Sweden?
Hi England! I hope we can share information concerning the other, and perhaps even work together in the future. Best of luck this game!

Hey Austria!

I think that sounds great, and best of luck to you as well!

Hi Germany! I hope we can work together, especially since we can work together pretty easily given our locations on the map. I will let you know if I hear anything regarding you. Best of luck this game!

Hey Germany!

I am happy to work with you as well and very much would like to work together. I don't know about Sweden yet, but I'll have a better idea once I see how people open. I assume you would like to be the one to take Belgium?

How do you feel about the Western Triple?

Hey Turkey!

I hope we can share information this game and even work together a little later. Best of luck!

Hey Italy!

I hope we can share information this game and even work together a little later. Best of luck!
I'm happy to not move to the channel this turn, but can we agree to DMZ it in the future?
Great, I'll be in touch.
Thanks!

Hey Germany! I'm very much the same - the GE is one of my favourite alliances, so I'm more than happy to help you make it work! I would of course like to take Sweden and I hope you will allow me to do so in the Autumn, I'm happy to agree not to take any aggressive actions in return
I agree! Best of luck to you as well!
A DMZ in Burgundy sounds good. How do you suggest we split the low countries? You take Holland and I take Belgium?

Greetings!
We sure can! I just opened negotiations with Russia and Austria and will inform you if I find out more useful.
Hi Turkey! I would love to cooperate with you, and hope we both make it far enough that our two nations can cooperate more directly.
Sure, I'd agree to that
Hey Turkey! I'm very much the same - BLA bounce and then me moving SEV-RUM is a standard Juggernaut open, but I'm happy to keep it disguised if you want to do the same!
Hi Russia! I hope we can cooperate against our common neighbors, and good luck to you this game!
Hey Turkey! Good luck this game. As the two eastern powers I think we can easily work together to take out Russia. What are your thoughts?
Also happy to make the Armenian DMZ in that case
Great!
Hey France! I hope we can too, I'll try to give you some information on Germany's plans and England's
I will not challenge your positions unless forced to. Let’s see how the game unfolds.
So I'll go for a rather standard opening to Bulgaria and the Black Sea. Let's just keep Armenia DMZ for now, then we'll work out how to make it look like we're at war.
Sounds good!
A DMZ in Piedmont could be acceptable, provided it does not restrict my future options. I remain focused on consolidating my own position.
I'm not against it at all, what do you think?
I appreciate your offer of cooperation, but I must remain cautious. For now, I will focus on securing my own supply centers.
I agree! I will absolutely let you into Sweden.
That sounds great. I hope we can have an amicable relationship.
Agreed. I see no reason for conflict at this stage.
Thanks! I will let you know what I hear as well.
I appreciate your support of the idea of an amicable relationship. I will remain cautious, but I see no reason why we cannot coexist without conflict for now.
I'm in! You could go for Serbia and Rumania in the spring, and I'll go for Bulgaria and the Black Sea. How does that sound to you?
Great! I'll let you know if I hear anything from your neighbors.
Good to hear. I'll open standard, and then we'll see what we can do in the fall.
That's what I was thinking!
So will I. I don't really have anything of note to say at the moment.
Are you gonna talk with Germany about Galicia?
Sounds good. I wish you the best of luck in your opening.

Greetings!
I wanted to open diplomatic channels with you. I doubt we'll have any interactions in the next few years, but maybe we can share some information.
Great!
Hello! I would love to share information with you! Do you have anything in mind at the moment?
I haven't heard much from your side of the board but I'll be sure to keep you in the loop!
I've been talking with most players around, and people are not really revealing their plans. But I will let you know if I learn anything.
I appreciate your cooperation. Should your movements near Piedmont or through the Alps become more aggressive, I will need to reassess. For now, I remain focused on Italy.
Did you speak to Germany? Do you have any idea where he is heading?
Sounds good, and I will do the same.
Your plans sound reasonable. I will maintain a neutral stance toward your operations in the eastern regions unless they threaten my interests.
Sounds good to me!
Awesome, I appreciate it
Sounds good.
Thanks for the information!
Hey Russia! As a traditional eastern power I think we could do very well together. What do you think about an alliance and how can I help you?

Hey Austria! I'm very much the same, so I'm happy to see that through with you! The first thing to do is to start with GAL. The most important issue for us is to get you a SER and me a RUM this year, so I'd propose a standard GAL bounce (though this is of course your territory and I don't mind if you prefer a DMZ there). I think the second key is to get you GRE. I'd suggest offering support to Italy, and letting them know that the Lepanto is off the table. Then you can promise to support them to BUL from SER, allowing you to move to GRE, blocking Turkey from getting 2 builds in the Balkans!
Nice! Yes that sounds great to me!

I am not opposed to bouncing in Galicia but I am curious if there are better options. What do you think about moving the army to Ukraine and then taking Rumania in the fall? Or are you wanting to go to Galicia to open with?
Good!
Do you know what Russia is doing with the army in Moscow?
I have not spoken to Germany directly, but I expect they will focus on securing their own centers first. I will keep an eye on their movements and update you if anything changes.
We agreed to a bounce in the Black Sea. I'm guessing he'll move Moscow - Ukraine and Warsaw - Galicia.
Sorry for the delayed response, I think we will be able to form a better strategy against Turkey if we don't bounce in Galicia.

Hey Italy!

I'm hoping we can be allies this game. I haven't heard much from your side of the board yet, but I'll be sure to keep you in the loop if I learn anything.
Thanks!
Okay that's what I'm expecting.
Sure, I'm happy with that. WAR will hold then
So you're gonna move to Galicia, Albania and Serbia?
Right
Perfect.
How are your conversations with Italy going? I haven't heard anything from him, which worries me.
Italy seems to be friendly, but I can't really tell what he's up to.

I have not yet made plans beyond securing my own supply centers and maintaining stability in southern Europe. If your ambitions align with mine, we may find opportunities later. For now, I remain cautious and focused on Italy.
Should you require support against France or Austria in the future, let me know. For now, I have no immediate need or plan to act beyond my borders.
Yea that's my concern too. I messaged him about making a lepanto opening with you not work out so that we could just focus on russia, but haven't heard anything
Hey Italy! I know we don't have a ton of opportunity for alliance at the moment, but we can help each other out if we keep in touch and share information, and I hope we can make it work!
So England, I hope you are happy to agree not to land an army in Norway, as long as you leave the Norwegian Sea empty?
You are welcome. I will continue to avoid unnecessary conflict in the east unless my interests are directly threatened.
Sounds good! I hope we can work together soon!

I appreciate your message. While our positions are distant, I remain focused on securing Italy and its surrounding regions. If your future moves threaten my interests, I will act accordingly. For now, I see no need for conflict.
Your message is noted. I have no immediate plans to expand beyond Italy, but I remain observant of your movements. Should opportunities arise later, I will keep your words in mind.

I'm glad to hear that.
I will be opening to Bulgaria and the Black Sea. Then we'll see what the autumn brings.

Opening toward Bulgaria is an ambitious move. I hope your path remains clear of any threats to your southern flank. I will continue to monitor events closely and remain neutral unless my own interests are at stake.
Italy's response to me was "let's see how the opening goes". I have a bad feeling about this.
Sounds great!
Thanks for the heads-up. I also have to watch Austria closely. I think he might be up to something.
Austria is indeed unpredictable. If his moves begin to threaten your flank or mine, I will consider adjusting my stance. For now, I remain focused on Italy.
I reckon Austria will open to Rumania and Serbia. Russia and I agreed to a bounce in the Black Sea.
Of course.
That should give you some time to focus on France, or perhaps turn against Germany.

I appreciate your insight on Austria's likely moves. While I remain focused on securing Italy and its immediate surroundings, I will keep an eye on his actions in the Balkans and their implications. My support is not guaranteed, but I remain open to future cooperation if interests align.
I understand that perfectly. Austria might also need time to develop. Good luck!
Let me know if you hear anything.
Will do.
Yes! I will not put an army in Norway and I will leave the Norwegian Sea empty.
I like it, but France needs to be onboard.
Absolutely.
How do you feel about a WT?
Of course, that makes sense.

Cool.

So far I have heard that Russia and Turkey are going to be friendly. I don't have a ton of details though.

Have you heard anything?
Cool! Thanks!

I heard the same from turkey and have been friendly with russia so far but turkey isn't really sharing a ton of info with me so I'm trying to be careful there. Do you plan on working with russia or germany?
I haven't decided yet. Still in the beginning stages.
Got it. Will you let me know what you end up deciding?
Perfect!
I'll reach out to him about it.
England brought up the idea of a Western Triple to me, what are your thoughts?
Thank you, I will do the same.
A Western Triple would be interesting. I have never done one before.
I would be open to it. Have you heard from England yet? I am not sure if they will go through with it.
It's pretty cool, but you do need to trust Germany a lot.
He seems interested!
Yes, of course!
Great!
Agreed. Have you spoken with Germany about this yet?
I have and I think he's in.
Great. Sounds like a plan.
Great.
I will take Iberia this year and go for Belgium the next.
Awesome!
He said you were onboard so I assume he is too!
I am onboard as well.
Sounds like a plan to me.
Sounds good!
So France says he wants to take Iberia this year and go for Belgium next year.
That's perfectly fine with me!
Perfect!
Should I be concerned by Germany's move into Ruhr?
I trust we are still on to do a WT? I think it will be in our best interests. I will take Belgium this year and then move toward Iberia next year.
Yep! I agree! You'll get Belgium this turn, then you can start to move toward Italy.
I'm confused as well. Did you agree to this?
What's with the move to Ruhr?
Good work avoiding the French stab!
Sounds good!
Hey! I think Germany is heading for a triple build. If you bounce him in Holland, I think we can convince France to bounce him in Belgium.
Why do you say that?
Has Germany said anything?

I have no interest in turning against Germany at this stage. My focus is on consolidating my position and ensuring stability within Italy. If your strategy shifts toward France or Austria in the future, we may find common ground.
Nice, looks like you got yourself an alliance with germany
Sounds good! Best of luck in the east!

He has said that he is still onboard with the WT and he plans to go for Belgium and Holland this year. He also said that he would move to Tyrolia and move toward Italy after getting his builds. We will see.
Yeah, although that move to Ruhr is a little concerning.
Do you want to go for Russia together?
Do you know something I don't?
Hey Germany! Nice opening, it looks like you have a nice alliance with England going. Hopefully we can work together once we figure out how the east is going.
Hey, what's with the move to Tyrolia?
Hey Russia, nice opening! What are your thoughts on Rumania? Turkey has a strong opening as well. Have you worked out what's going on in the north?
Yes I think that will work well!
Are you attacking Germany?
Has he asked for your support into Belgium?
I think it is fine that France is in Bur. If anything, it will probably help you later with Austria or Italy.
Yeah, hopefully you can keep him from trying to do anything crazy.
EC is always an aggressive opening move
Nice opening! Looks like you have good options to pick between Germany and England. Have you decided who to ally with?
Agreed, let me know how you are feeling about England and France.

Your plans to act with Russia in the Black Sea are intriguing. I will continue to monitor developments there, though I see no immediate threat to my interests. Should your strategy evolve and require southern support, let me know.
Not really. It doesn't concern me at the moment.
Nice opening! Looks like everything is in order. What are your thoughts on how we should proceed?
Thank you, but I prefer to keep things straightforward. My units remain in defensive positions, and I have no interest in aggressive expansion at this time. Let’s see how the northern front develops.
He hasn't said anything to me yet
How are your relations with Austria at the moment?
I'm thinking I'll just take Holland and Denmark and let France move to Burgundy
OK sounds good!
How do you want to proceed with Rumania?
Yeah, I guess. Hopefully no hard feelings!
It looks like you're heading for a triple build.
Interesting
No move toward Tyrolia was made, and I have no intention of engaging Germany directly. My focus remains on securing southern Italy and maintaining stability.
Yeah, that's what it looks like.
Is there anything you would like to see me do this turn? I am focusing on getting my two centers this turn.
This is going to be a fun first turn.
Yeah, WAR was a little bit of a random move on my part, but it should be fine. What are your thoughts on the Balkans?
Sounds good!
Okay. It just looked super aggressive toward Austria.
Yeah that's the hope!
Well, if you were bounced it would have been equally aggressive! It was purely defensive
Relations remain neutral and unchanged. I have no interest in aligning with or against any power at this stage. My focus is strictly on internal stability and defense.
Okay, cool.

I appreciate your interest in coordination, but I see no need for action beyond what I have already taken. My units are in secure positions, and I intend to remain neutral unless events force a change.
Yeah France and Russia haven't messaged me at all either. I am curious who they are trying to coordinate with.
Germany said he wants to move Ruhr to Holland.
What are your thoughts on Russia?
That makes sense, let's stay in touch
Yeah.
I talked to Germany, and he won't be moving to Holland. I can't really tell if he wants a triple build, but it seems he has set his sights on Belgium.
I reckon it's best to wait for next year, because if Russia doesn't build, his front against us will be completely exposed.
OK sounds good.
Italy told me that his move was purely defensive. I hope that is the truth.
Cool!
Great.
Have you gotten much from him? I haven't been able to get much out of him.
Have you heard much from Italy? He's been very quiet to me.
I haven't heard from him much. Have you?
No, he hasn't responded to me since before the phase.
I assure you, there is no aggression intended toward Austria or anyone else. My orders remain purely defensive. I will continue to avoid entanglements unless forced otherwise.
Thanks! I will try to ally with Germany. Looks like you are in a great position. Do you know if Italy plans to move to Greece?
He said he was just taking Tunis and keeping everything chill.
Not too much
Certainly. I have no interest in conflict, but if your movements near TRI or the Adriatic require coordination, feel free to reach out.
Italy is interested in Turkey. I would imagine he would want Tunis this turn.
He's definitely aiming for a triple build, as he's openly saying so.
Have you decided on who you are allying with?
Your expansion into Ukraine is noted. I remain neutral and focused on southern Italy, but should your future plans intersect with mine, I will not ignore them.
Same here.
What do you think about attacking Russia? It's probably a bit early but we could make short work of him if you put a little pressure in the north.
I'm guessing you're going to convoy to Norway?
Yeah, I think that is likely.
Do you know what Italy is up to?
I hope so.
I assume he would ask for my support. He hasn't asked, though I will let you know.
You might want to try to make nice with Turkey and go against Russia.
Can I assume that we still have a DMZ in the Channel?
Appreciate it. What do you think about a move to tyrolia? We could bounce and it would help me deal with Russia.
I don't know if he will try for Greece or not, which makes me a little nervous. What's your plan for Bulgaria?
Yeah, I'm planning to.
That's what we agreed.
I am a little nervous about Italy, I haven't heard much from him.
Austria has asked me to ally with you to attack Turkey, saying you are in a good position to attack him. Would you be interested?
I think you have to worry more about France.

A move to Tyrolia could be interesting, but I see no direct benefit to Italy at this moment. Until I observe clearer strategic advantages or threats, I will remain focused on securing southern Italy and maintaining stability.
I'm not planning to attack Russia yet.
Yeah, we do.
How would you like to proceed this turn? Italy is making me nervous.
Fair enough. Let's stay in touch.
Yep.
I'm currently unsure. It's either moving to Greece, or staying put.
Cool
Great! I wish you the best of luck this turn.
Yeah I am working on it. I think he is hesitant to commit yet.

An alliance to attack Turkey is premature without clear coordination or shared interest. I remain focused on my own stability and have no immediate plans to engage in foreign conflicts. Let me know if your strategy shifts.
Of course, as should be obvious!
Okay fair enough. Are you going for Tunis this turn?
I think I'll move to Greece.
Okay I'm thinking about moving to Rumania, if you move to Greece and Black Sea we will be in good shape against Russia.
If you attack Russia Turkey will have to attack him.
It doesn't look like Italy is interested, but I will let you know.
I heard England might go for Belgium and that Germany will be going for a triple build.
Agreed.
I'm currently trying to determine whether Russia is gonna try for Rumania or Black Sea. If he's trying for Black Sea, it could be more difficult for us.
Okay thanks. I haven't heard much from Italy, so I think he's just sitting back for now.
Okay. Let me know what you decide.
I was wondering if you could support me into Sweden next year?

I think the most important thing is to secure you a GRE, and me a RUM. You have to take a gamble for TRI/VIE or SER/GRE, but if you move to GRE with SER and support it with ALB then you are guaranteed to get one and you should be safe
Sounds good.
Nice
Could you move into Galicia?
Yeah I think that makes the most sense. Will you be taking Rumania with the fleet or with Ukraine?
Possibly. Germany has been noncommittal so far.
I would agree.
I'll let you know before the phase is over.
Are you planning on going for Greece?
They could have just been lying to me then.
Possibly.
Ukraine
We'll see after the phase is over.
Indeed. I wish you luck this turn.
You too!
Tunis is not currently on my radar. My focus remains on securing southern Italy and maintaining defensive stability. I see no immediate reason to pursue expansion at this stage.
Indeed. I remain focused on my own consolidation and will avoid entanglements unless circumstances demand otherwise.
Greece is a distant concern for now. I have no immediate plans to expand beyond securing my own supply centers. I will keep your interests in mind if the situation evolves.

Hey I'm sorry I lied about letting you into Sweden, but I'm thinking I might need it. I know you probably don't want to hear this, but I'll tell you what I plan to do:
I'm moving to Sweden, but I can give it back to you next year.
Great. I wish you the best of luck this turn!
Likewise—I remain focused on stability and consolidation. Should your plans evolve and require coordination, feel free to reach out.
I'm sorry I thought I would be in a better position, but I don't think I am.

Well, that's very disappointing. I can't really accept that, I have no intention of moving against you but I'm still going to do that. I have no intention of moving against you either, so I'd like to discuss this with you. A 6 center Germany in the first year is hardly conducive to an alliance with Russia...
OK, thanks.

The best that you can do this turn is to stay in Denmark, not move to SWE. I'm not quite sure why you think you need an extra build against me when you have this French unit in BUR and England going strong for Belgium. However, I still have more units up north than England does and if you give me the build, you don't need to worry about me taking advantage of the situation. It would still be more than happy to work together in the future, but your hostile attitude is also very poor...
Do you have any insight into what Russia is planning?
Are you happy to bounce again, or are you going to move to CON to protect against a potential Lepanto?
Thanks.

I'm not sure. I think I'll move to Con.
What are you gonna do with Warsaw?
WAR will hold, most likely
OK, so you're not gonna move to Galicia?
No, I'd rather move to UKR in case Austria does something unexpected
Do you have any information concerning England or Germany?
OK
Nothing really, Germany is going to bounce me out of SWE though, so I'd suggest you go for Munich
Just letting you know, so you don't think I'm going back on what I've said
And also a heads up on what I'm hearing about Italy's plans (probably nothing though), but just letting you know.
Thanks. I'll see what happens this turn.
Sure thing
Thanks, I'll let you know if I hear anything interesting.
Sure thing!
Have you decided what to do about Rumania?

Russia’s movements into Ukraine suggest a focus on Eastern Europe for now. I see no direct threat to your interests, but I will continue to monitor their strategy closely. If their ambitions shift southward, I will let you know.
Thanks.
Yeah, I'll be moving there
Cool
It's nothing against you personally, I just think this gives me a better chance at Belgium.
Belgium is not guaranteed, and England can contest it
That's true, and I've just had a lot of Russian players stab me when I did this, I just want to make sure.
Could you bounce England in Norway?
I think I can, I'll do it on the condition that you don't bounce me in Sweden
Okay, thank you!
No worries!

AUSTRIA: A SER S F ALB - GRE
ENGLAND: F ENG C A WAL - BEL
ITALY: A ROM S F NAP - APU

messages.message: It looks like Germany decided to bounce you out of Belgium. I am building two armies. Do you want me to support you into Belgium next turn?
What are you gonna build?
I can try, but it probably won't work.
I don't need support, but thanks! I'm planning to take Belgium.
I'll support you into Ruhr if you want.
What are you planning to build?
You're welcome. I remain focused on maintaining stability within Italy. If your strategy shifts toward consolidation or expansion, we can reassess future coordination.
Well, you got me to believe it. Nice move.
Are you gonna build an army in Sev?

I remain focused on securing southern Italy and monitoring regional stability. My adjustments will reinforce my core centers, ensuring they remain impenetrable. I have no immediate plans beyond consolidation.
Where are you planning to build?
Sounds good. I will focus on consolidating this year.
OK.
So, how do we proceed from here?
I appreciate your attention, but I am not here to entertain theatrics or distractions. My focus is on securing Italy’s interests quietly and efficiently.
Okay, fair enough.
Well, I'm not gonna build a fleet in SEV if that is what you are asking!
Are you working with Italy?
I remain focused on reinforcing southern Italy and will keep my options open for the future. If your consolidation efforts align with regional stability, I’ll keep our dialogue open.
Ok, thanks.
I appreciate your acknowledgment. My strategy remains unchanged—I will build strength quietly and ensure southern Italy remains secure.
I assume your ‘okay’ implies acceptance of my stance. I will continue to pursue Italy’s interests without interference or spectacle.
Great.
Why would you lie to me on this? I can just take back Trieste right now.
Yeah, that's what I assumed.
Okay, so what do you think for builds? I think Russia is going to build fleet Sev, that should make our task easier.
No I am not. I expected to bounce Italy in Trieste and this is unfortunate.
I'm going to build one fleet and one army, in Berlin and Munich.
Cool, I'll build a fleet in London and an army in Edi.

I do not understand the nature of your accusation. I have not lied, nor have I made claims that invite aggression. Trieste remains unmentioned in my strategy, and I advise you not to assume Italy’s inaction as weakness.

Yeah, that was unfortunate.

Has Germany said anything about his intentions?
Your ‘great’ is noted. If your future moves involve southern Italy or any alignment with my defensive priorities, I remain open to dialogue—provided it is substantive.
That's my guess, too.
Good!
Your ‘okay’ is accepted. I will continue to pursue Italy’s quiet consolidation without interference or further commentary.
I am not sure. He is going for Russia and not sure beyond that. He says he is just sitting on his hands until an opportunity presents itself.

OK.
What are you planning to build?
Interesting.
Okay I will go for Greece and you take Rumania and we can start pushing north.
I guess we will see who Italy is working with after the builds.
Sounds good!

I am not accusing you of anything, but I don't understand the point of not taking Tunis, especially if you were so concerned with the stability of the south. I don't see any strategic reason for you to move to Tyrolia.
What are your plans for the north?
I intend to build an army in VENETIA to reinforce southern Italy’s stability. My focus remains on consolidation and ensuring that our mutual southern flank remains secure.
So I was thinking of moving on St. Petersburg this year. Are you good with that?
Sorry I didn't see that until after the phase went through.

Tunis is irrelevant to Italy’s current priorities, and Tyrolia was moved to assert influence where it matters—away from France and closer to securing my northern flank. I suggest you focus on your own ambitions without questioning mine.
Italy said they were focused on southern stability and keeping their options open, so I would not expect an immediate attack from them.
I'm thinking of attacking Russia.
Sounds good.
You?
Thanks. I will let you know if I have any immediate plans to move south.
I agree, I think he is trying to create a conflict between Russia and I. It will be interesting to see who he is working with.
I assume your '?' is a request for clarity. My strategy is straightforward: consolidate Italy’s supply centers and avoid entanglements. I will continue to do so without fanfare or interference.
Sounds good.
I don't think I will be able to this year, but I will try and come in from the south as soon as I can.
It's fine, it worked out anyway.
OK
Understood. If your movement south becomes imminent, I will appreciate early notice so I may adjust accordingly. Southern Italy remains a priority for stability.
Of course! I'll take Warsaw.
How are things shaping up for you in the north?
Acknowledged. Should your future moves align with securing the eastern Mediterranean or southern Balkans, I remain open to discussing mutual stability—provided it suits Italy’s interests.
I understand. It is good to have clarity.
Okay, so after seeing Russia's moves, are you thinking you will build an army?
Great!
Clarity is efficient. I will continue to pursue Italy’s consolidation without theatrics or interference. If your path remains disengaged, I have no further need for commentary.
Italy said that his move to Tyrolia was defensive and he wanted to stay in a defensive position, but after seeing his moves this turn I don't trust him.

I appreciate that.
Good luck!
Okay that's fair. What are you looking at for this turn?
Yeah.
I'm thinking of a fleet in Ankara. I will still move to Rumania in the autumn.
So I will be moving on St. Petersburg this year. I think Germany will be moving on Warsaw.
Sounds good.
Okay that sounds good to me.
Okay sounds good.

I’m looking to build an army in VENETIA to strengthen southern Italy’s defenses. My focus remains on consolidation and ensuring stability along my northern flank. No aggressive moves are planned at this time.
Are you thinking fleet or army in Marseilles?
Cool.
Cool.
I'll try to get Austria's help.

I appreciate your candor. I think it will make sense for you to build in Venice as it's a better defensive position. I would prefer that you didn't build an army in Rome, it will just make it more difficult for me to trust you.
I would probably still make sure to defend Trieste, but you could take Galicia.
Are you considering a move against France?
That would be great.
Sorry, what are you thinking for your build?

TRI remains a strategic center under my control. I suggest you focus on your own expansion goals rather than questioning mine.
Yes.
Do you think that England and Italy are working together?
Yes, but I'm not sure.
You should tell Germany to attack Tyrolia.
Why?
I'm trying to decide. I might attack Italy, or I might try to take SER and TRI.
They probably won't.
Your assurance is noted, but I remain focused on securing southern Italy and the western Mediterranean. If your actions contradict this peace, the consequences will fall on your shoulders.
OK.
I have no interest in attacking you, but if your fleet moves toward ROM or NAP, you will find me prepared.
You might want to try to convince Turkey to support you.
I'm not sure yet.
Why did you move back into the Aegean Sea?
TRI remains under my control and will remain so. My focus is southern Italy and the western Mediterranean.
I'm thinking about building a fleet in Berlin.
I'm just making sure that you won't be supporting Austria.
My fleet will not move into any Italian province.
Why?
Your assurance is noted, but I remain focused on securing southern Italy and the western Mediterranean. If your actions contradict this peace, the consequences will fall on your shoulders.
Turkey has been talking about attacking you.
I have no interest in supporting Austria, nor do I intend to interfere with your expansion goals—provided they remain outside my sphere of control, particularly around TRI.
I'm trying to convince Italy that I'm not going to attack him.
Are you going to move SPA-LYO and MAO-SPA this turn?
Your fleet's current inaction is noted, but I remain prepared. Should your fleet move toward ROM or NAP, expect a firm response.
I'm not planning to attack you.
I'm not sure yet.
I could take BEL if England doesn't move into the English Channel.
What are you planning to build?
I was just making sure.

Your assurance is noted, but I expect the same clarity from your allies. TRI remains under my control and will remain so. I suggest you focus on securing your own supply centers rather than questioning mine.
Understood.
Why do you keep repeating the same thing?
I have no immediate plans for expansion beyond securing my current holdings and Tunis. My focus remains on southern Italy and the western Mediterranean. I expect no interference in that sphere.
It would be useful in the fight against England.
OK
I remain focused on securing southern Italy and the western Mediterranean. Provided your ambitions remain outside my sphere, particularly around TRI, I see no reason for conflict.
What are you going to build?
Ok, so I shouldn't tell you that I'm not going to attack you, then?

Your question suggests a lack of clarity about my intentions. I have made it clear—I will not initiate aggression, nor will I tolerate threats to my holdings, especially TRI. If your actions remain neutral toward me, I see no reason for conflict. But I expect the same clarity from your allies.
Ok, that makes sense.
I intend to reinforce my southern holdings and secure Tunis. Provided your actions remain neutral toward me, I see no reason for conflict. I expect no interference in my sphere of influence.
What are you planning to do?
Ok.
I don't know yet.

I appreciate your acknowledgment. My focus remains on securing southern Italy and the western Mediterranean. Provided your ambitions remain outside my sphere, particularly around TRI, I see no reason for conflict. However, any aggressive moves toward my borders will be met with firm resistance.
I might move into TRI and BUD.
Why?
I'm not going to make any aggressive moves towards you.

Your assurance is noted, but actions remain the only measure of intent. I expect your fleet to remain neutral toward my holdings, particularly TRI. I have no interest in conflict, but I will defend my borders decisively.
Do you think you can support me into Tyrolia?

Support into Tyrolia is not in my immediate interests. My focus remains on securing southern Italy and Tunis. I expect no interference in my sphere, but I have no plans to assist your northern campaigns.
As I told you before, I'm not going to attack you.
OK, that makes sense.
Your fleet’s inaction is noted, but I remain prepared. Should you move toward NAP or ROM, expect decisive resistance. I have no desire for conflict, but I will defend my holdings.

My focus remains southern Italy and the western Mediterranean. Provided your ambitions remain outside my sphere, particularly around TRI, I see no reason for conflict. Any aggressive moves toward my borders will be met with firm resistance.

Italy is starting to get on my nerves.
I think that you should try to take Greece.

Your assurance is noted, but actions remain the only measure of intent. I expect your fleet to remain neutral toward my holdings, particularly TRI. I have no interest in conflict, but I will defend my borders decisively.
I intend to reinforce my southern holdings and secure Tunis. Provided your actions remain neutral toward me, I see no reason for conflict. I expect no interference in my sphere.

I have no interest in supporting moves into Tyrolia at this time. My focus remains on securing southern Italy and the western Mediterranean. Provided your actions remain neutral toward me, I see no reason for conflict, but I cannot offer assistance where my interests do not align.
I'm thinking about it.
I would suggest doing it in the fall turn.
What are you planning to do?

Your assurance is noted, but as always, actions will determine the truth of your intent. I remain focused on securing southern Italy and the western Mediterranean, and TRI remains firmly under my control. Provided your fleet remains neutral toward my holdings, I see no reason for conflict. But I will defend my borders decisively if challenged.
I see that you're still occupying Tyrolia.
So, you don't believe that I'm not going to attack you.
If you let me move into Tyrolia, I won't attack you and I'll help you against Austria.
I won't move my fleet towards you.
I'm going to build a fleet in Berlin.

I hope you will keep our agreement.

messages.message: It looks like you are going to win.
You should disband your army in STP so that you can keep the fleet in MAO
Do you want to move into MUN?

Your acknowledgment is accepted. I intend to reinforce my southern holdings and secure Tunis this phase. Provided your actions remain neutral toward me, I see no reason for conflict. I expect no interference in my sphere, but I have no further interest in your northern campaigns.
I think that you should invade Germany.
You don't need to worry about me attacking Italy.
Do you trust me now?
Are you going to build in BER and MUN?
I'm sorry about that. I tried to support you into SER, but you didn't do that attack
What happened in Spain?
I moved out of the Aegean.

I heard your words, but I remain cautious. VEN is vital to me, and I will not rely on assurances alone. If your actions align with peace, Italy will reciprocate—until then, I will act in self-defense.
I am not going to attack you.
Your movements are noted, but trust is earned through action, not words. I will monitor your fleet closely. If your path leads toward Italy, I will not hesitate to defend what is mine.
You should build a fleet in TRI
So, do you trust me now?
That would put me at risk of losing TRI.
I might help you against Austria, though.
You can support hold it from SER and VIE
But I am going to build a fleet anyway.
France is still attacking you

Trust is earned through action, not rhetoric. Your fleet remains in the eastern Mediterranean, and your alliance with Austria is clear. Until I see proof of disengagement from their strategy, I will treat your movements toward Italy as hostile.
My plan is to move into the Adriatic.
It looks like Turkey is going to attack you.
It doesn't really look like Turkey is going to attack me
I have no interest in entanglements unless they directly benefit me. If your offer of help is genuine, it must come with proof of action—not vague promises.
France has not attacked me directly. If you believe they have, provide clear evidence. I will not act on secondhand warnings or vague accusations.
Are you going to help me attack England?
I moved into the Adriatic.
I can assure you that I will not attack Italy.
I'm pretty sure France is going to build a fleet in MAR. They can use that to attack VEN
I forgot to change my orders.

Your movement into the Adriatic is noted. I trust in action, not intent. Should your fleet approach my centers, I will not hesitate to defend them. I expect the same from you—if our borders meet, I will expect respect.
I might be able to do that
Your assurance is recorded, but I remain cautious. VEN is vital to me, and I will not rely on words alone to protect it. If your actions do not reflect peace, I will respond accordingly.
I tried to support you, but you moved into TRI
Maybe they won't. I am not sure what Turkey's intentions are.
What do you mean?
Oh.

Your warning about France is noted, but I require more than speculation. If France does move aggressively toward VEN, I will act to defend it. Until then, I will not be swayed by third-party concerns.
Italy might try to move their fleet into the Western Mediterranean.
I might build in Munich and Berlin.

Turkey took SER
What do you plan to do?
I would appreciate if you didn't help them
I understand.
I can defend it.
What do you expect me to do?
I can't help them.
I might be able to do it
I think you should build a fleet in KIE and an army in MUN
Why not?

I expect nothing from you—only that you remain neutral toward me. Your focus is the north; my focus is the south. If your actions do not interfere with my holdings or ambitions, I see no reason for conflict. Let others bleed themselves in their wars. I will remain silent unless provoked.
I'll build in Munich and Berlin.
I think I will do that.
If I make no other aggressive move, will you move out of the Adriatic?
Maybe you should talk to Italy and see if Italy wants to help you
Ok.

If your movement remains peaceful and does not threaten my Adriatic holdings, I will reassess my position. Until then, TRI remains Italy’s concern. I expect no interference—only proof of disengagement.
I have an agreement with Turkey.
It's not speculation. France is moving his army from BUR into MAR
What are you going to do?
What do you think you'll do after you take Norway?
OK.
I'm going to help you attack England

I expect nothing from you. Your neutrality toward Italy is clear, and I will not ask for more. Let your northern conflicts consume themselves while I secure my southern interests. If your actions remain non-threatening, we will not interfere.
I'll support you into NOR
I don't know yet.
France moving into MAR does not change my stance. I will monitor their movements closely and act only if they threaten VEN or TRI. Until then, I will not be swayed by third-party concerns.
What are you thinking about doing now?
He's probably going to betray you
Do you want me to support your fleet in the Black Sea into RUM?
What are you going to do now?
I will do so if you do not move into SER.
So I should move out of the Adriatic and leave TRI?
I can't afford to attack Turkey right now.

TRI remains Italy’s concern, and I will not abandon it lightly. If you move out of the Adriatic voluntarily and do not attempt to reclaim it, I may consider withdrawing pressure—though I will still maintain a watchful stance. Action must precede any discussion of mutual restraint.
I'm going to attack England

I understand your claim that France is moving into MAR, but I have observed no direct threat to VEN or TRI from their actions. I will continue to monitor their fleet closely, especially near the Tyrrhenian Sea. If your intelligence proves reliable and France escalates, I will act accordingly. Until then, I will not be drawn into conflict based on third-party warnings.
Do you know what you're going to do with your armies in Germany?
What are you going to do with your fleet in the Adriatic then?
I'll try that
I'm probably going to attack England.
If I move out of the Adriatic and don't take TRI, will you retreat GRE-ALB?
Italy won't attack me.

It will remain in the Adriatic.
I'm going to take BER

Ok

I'll support you into TRI.
Thanks

Where will you move from?
Thank you.
Do you want me to attack Italy then?
Russia is going to attack you.
England is a threat to the stability of Europe.
OK.
TRI remains Italy’s concern, but I am willing to reassess if your fleet moves away from my Adriatic flank. Until then, I will maintain my presence and readiness.

I do not concern myself with England’s ambitions unless they threaten me directly. VEN remains my priority. If your actions align with peace and do not press toward TRI or VEN, Italy will not interfere. But do not mistake silence for submission.
My fleet will remain in the Adriatic.
I could support you into Spain if you support me into Portugal afterwards
Yes.
Or do you want to attack Turkey?
What are you planning to build?
What are you going to do after we attack England?
From Serbia or Budapest?
France told me that England is planning to move into the Western Mediterranean.
The only reason I moved into the Adriatic was because you attacked me in GRE.

I have no interest in revealing my build plans. My focus remains on securing southern Italy and the western Mediterranean. As long as your actions remain neutral toward me, I see no reason for conflict. Do not expect cooperation, only non-interference.
So, I hope that you understand.

France’s claims about England are irrelevant to me unless they threaten VEN or TRI. I will not be drawn into third-party conflicts. If your actions remain peaceful and do not press toward Italy’s holdings, I will reciprocate. But I trust nothing—not even silence.
I will move from BUD.
Are you going to support TRI with VEN then?
I will not attack VEN or TRI.

You moved into the Adriatic after my actions in GRE. That alone makes your claims of peaceful intent hollow. If you truly seek peace, withdraw from TRI’s flank and stay out. Until then, I will treat your presence as hostile.
I think we should try to convince England to attack Italy or Austria.
Are you going to move your army in RUM to BUD?

Understood. But let me be equally clear: TRI remains Italy’s concern. If your fleet continues to press toward my borders, I will respond accordingly. Words mean little—actions will define our relationship.
I do not.

I have no interest in supporting TRI with VEN. My focus is on securing TUN and maintaining the integrity of my southern holdings. If Austria threatens TRI, I will act to defend it, but I expect no interference from others.
OK
I know.
You are my only ally.
I was just thinking that you should attack Russia.

Your assurance is noted, but I will judge your actions—not your words. VEN is vital to me, and I will not rely on empty promises to protect it. If you move toward TRI or VEN, I will respond decisively.
It looks like I am going to attack Italy.
Ok, I'll support you from SER.

I'll move out of the Adriatic.

Words alone do not convince. If you are not attacking TRI or VEN next turn, your fleet must remain distant and your army must not pressure TRI or VEN. I will watch closely. Silence means little—I expect proof.

I am not going to attack you unless you attack me first.
You need to protect TRI from Italy.
Your build plans are of no concern to me, provided your actions remain neutral toward Italy’s holdings. I expect no interference in the south, and I will respond only if your neutrality is broken.

I have no intention of withdrawing from ALB unless you do so first and prove it permanent. Until your fleet moves away from TRI and the Adriatic, I will treat your presence as hostile.
So, you don't want to move out of ALB.
I don't think you can take VEN with the fleet in ALB.
I do not want to move out of ALB. If you wish for peace, you must move out of TRI’s flank first. Action must come from you, not words. Until then, I will treat your presence as hostile.
OK, thanks.

Do you want me to support your army in MUN?
It would be helpful if you attacked Holland.
You should move into Burgundy and Picardy.

If you move to Burgundy and Picardy, you can attack Belgium.
Sorry, I meant Burgundy and the Ruhr.

Turkey will probably betray me soon.

But I have been trusting them so far.
If Turkey betrays you soon, it may create an opening for Italy in the Adriatic. We can discuss coordination if the balance shifts.
Turkey and I had an agreement about how to split the Balkans.
But that doesn't mean that I trust them.

I hear your concerns about Turkey's reliability. If their actions threaten TRI or VEN, I would be open to discussing mutual defense arrangements—though I will need to see concrete proof of their aggression before committing any forces eastward.
Turkey and I have had an agreement to split the Balkans. That has worked well so far, but I am wary of Turkey.

I appreciate your concerns about Turkey’s reliability. I remain open to discussing coordination if circumstances along the Adriatic change, particularly if Turkey's fleet moves closer to TRI or if their ambitions threaten VEN. For now, I will maintain a neutral stance and continue to focus on securing my western flank.
I want to attack Berlin, but I would need to attack Munich first.
I understand.

I understand your skepticism toward Turkey, and I share it. Should their fleet commit to the Adriatic or attempt to pressure TRI or VEN, I will consider supporting your interests there—though I will not commit until I see clear evidence of their aggression. Until then, I will remain focused westward.
I will be able to tell soon whether or not Turkey will attack me.
You could move the army in TRI to TYR, then you would have two armies in place to take MUN.
Will you support me into Serbia?

If Turkey’s aggression becomes undeniable, I will reconsider my stance on the Adriatic. For now, I remain focused on protecting TRI and VEN, and I expect you to do the same. If you require support against Turkey, I will need clear proof of their intent before I act.
I'm trying to convince Germany to attack England.
I can't afford to attack you or Turkey.

I expect nothing from you and ask for nothing in return. But if Turkey moves decisively toward TRI or threatens VEN, I will expect a clear signal from you before I act. Until then, I remain focused westward.
You should do VEN->TRI

Yes, I'll support you into SER from RUM
I am too worried about Turkey.

OK.

If you do not move into TRI, I will remain focused on securing TUN and defending my own centers. I have no interest in unnecessary conflict, but I will not act against you unless you threaten TRI or VEN.

I expect nothing from you and ask for nothing in return. But if Turkey moves decisively toward TRI or threatens VEN, I will expect a clear signal from you before I act. Until then, I remain focused westward.
Are you going to do AEG->GRE?

What are you going to do about Turkey?
Can you support me into SER?

I have no interest in supporting movement into SER at this time. My focus remains westward, on securing TUN and ensuring TRI remains unthreatened. Any action toward SER must come from Turkey independently, and I will not divert forces or support from my own priorities.
I appreciate your flexibility.
I do not know.
OK.

I appreciate your flexibility and understanding. If Turkey’s fleet moves decisively toward TRI or threatens VEN, I will expect a clear signal from you before I act. Until then, I remain focused westward.
I am sorry that I betrayed you.

Can you move SWE into SKA so that you can support yourself into NOR?
England is a threat to the stability of Europe.
I am also trying to convince Russia.
Do you want to help us fight England?

Why do you want me to move to SKA?
Try to defend myself from Turkey.

I have no forces available to support movement into SER at this time. My focus remains westward, on securing TUN and ensuring TRI remains unthreatened. Any action toward SER must come from Turkey independently, and I will not divert forces or support from my own priorities.
I am not sure yet.
You are right. I meant that I am also trying to convince Germany to fight England.
So you're not supporting me into SER?
I could attack Berlin, but I would need to take Munich first.
I don't want to fight England

I appreciate your candor. Until Turkey’s fleet commits to the Adriatic or threatens TRI or VEN, I will remain focused westward and maintain neutrality in eastern affairs. If conditions change, I expect a clear signal from you before I consider any further action.
I will attack MUN with help from France.
I'll move out of the Adriatic.
You can attack GRE from AEG
So that you can attack England

Your plan to attack MUN with French support is concerning. I will not interfere unless it draws Turkey’s attention toward TRI or VEN. If your actions risk destabilizing our mutual flank, I expect a clear warning beforehand.
Will you move out of the Ionian Sea?
I'll support you
If Turkey attacks me, it will be apparent that they are betraying me and will be necessary for me to attack them.
If you move out of the Adriatic, I expect no new incursions into TRI or VEN. My neutrality is not unconditional—if your fleet reappears near TRI without warning, I will reassess my stance.
OK
Yes.
I will not attack you or France.
Ok.

Your assurance not to attack me or France is noted, but I remain focused on securing TRI and VEN. If Turkey’s actions force your hand, I expect a clear signal before I adjust my stance. Until then, I will remain westward.
You can expect a clear signal when Turkey makes their move.
I will attack MUN. Will you support me?
My fleet is moving ADR-TRI
I think I can get into the North Sea by myself.
I can't right now, because I have to defend myself from England.
What are you going to build?
But I will help you soon.
Your agreement is noted, but I remain focused on securing TUN and ensuring TRI remains unthreatened. If your fleet reappears near TRI or VEN without warning, I will reassess my neutrality.

Your assurance of a clear signal is noted. I remain focused on securing TRI and VEN. If Turkey’s fleet moves toward TRI or threatens my flank, I expect your signal promptly—Italy does not act on speculation.
Ok
Deal.
An army in Kiel.
Are you going to help me attack Austria?
They have a unit in VEN and an army in TYR
I am not planning to help you attack Austria.
I don't want Austria to collapse too quickly, because I'm worried about Italy and Turkey.
Why not?
I'm going to help you defend Austria.
I think we should try to convince England to attack Italy.
I'm not going to help defend Austria. I'm going to help Austria attack Turkey

I'm not going to attack Austria either.

Your fleet movement toward TRI is concerning. I expect no aggressive action toward my supply centers. If your fleet remains near TRI without clear justification, I will reconsider my stance on neutrality.

I will not move ADR-VEN.
I'll continue to help defend Austria
OK, I will not attack Austria.
But I'm worried about Turkey
I'm going to build an army in Constantinople
Me too.
Are you going to try to convince Italy to attack Turkey?
I'm trying to convince Austria to attack Turkey
Austria is going to attack Turkey anyway.

Your assurance not to move toward ADR or VEN is noted, but remember—I expect no action from your fleet near TRI without warning. If your fleet reappears in the Adriatic, I will reassess my neutrality. Until then, I remain focused westward.

I'm building an army in MOS
To defend MOS

I think you should build a fleet in Kiel
Will you attack Turkey with me?

Nothing.
Are you going to help me attack England?
Why?
Do you want me to do something with it?

Italy has no interest in joining a campaign against Turkey at this time. My focus remains on securing Tunis and protecting TRI and VEN. Any aggressive moves toward the Adriatic will be met with a reassessment of my neutrality.
I can support you in BUD or something if you want
I want you to attack Austria
What are you going to do after you take Budapest?
Your assurance not to move into the Adriatic is noted. I expect that to remain true. If your fleet appears near TRI or threatens VEN without warning, I will reconsider my stance.
I might need help defending myself from England.
So you'll help me attack Austria?
Do you understand that we're having a standoff now?
I don't have the armies to fight England.

A standoff implies tension, not conflict. I remain focused on securing TUN and ensuring TRI remains unthreatened. If your fleet remains west of the Aegean and does not challenge me, we can maintain this balance. But if your fleet moves toward TRI or VEN without warning, I will reassess everything.
My fleet is not moving west into the Ionian Sea

Your assurances not to move into the Adriatic or toward the Ionian Sea are noted. I will accept them at face value—for now. If your fleet appears near TRI or VEN without warning, I will act accordingly.
I'm worried about your fleet in ALB and your army in GRE.

A standoff implies tension, not conflict. I remain focused on securing Tunis and ensuring TRI remains unthreatened. If your fleet remains west of the Aegean and does not challenge me, we can maintain this balance. But if your fleet moves toward TRI or VEN without warning, I will reassess everything.

Your concerns about my fleet in ALB and army in GRE are noted, but I remind you—my forces remain defensive and focused westward. I have no interest in the Aegean at this time. If you choose to act aggressively toward me, do not expect me to remain passive.
I don't want to attack you.

I understand your hesitation. I have no interest in conflict with you at this time, but I expect clarity about your fleet's movements. If you move westward, I will expect your fleet to remain west of the Aegean Sea and avoid the Adriatic altogether. If you do so, we can maintain the current balance. If you move toward TRI or VEN without warning, I will act accordingly.
I promise I won't move into the Ionian Sea
Your promise not to move into the Ionian Sea is noted. I expect no action from your fleet near TRI or VEN without warning. If your assurances are broken, do not expect me to remain passive.

If you intend to move westward, I suggest staying west of the Aegean Sea and avoiding the Adriatic altogether. I have no interest in conflict, but I will not tolerate aggressive moves toward TRI or VEN without warning.
I won't move into TRI or VEN
I am committed to peace with you.

I have no desire to attack you either, but I expect clarity about your fleet's future positioning. If your fleet moves westward, ensure it remains west of the Aegean Sea and does not threaten TRI or VEN.
Your assurance is noted, but I remain cautious. If your fleet remains west of the Aegean and does not threaten TRI or VEN, I will not act against your interests. However, I will monitor closely.
You should move your army out of ALB
I will attack Munich.
Your commitment to peace is appreciated, though I remain pragmatic. I will not interfere in your affairs north of VEN or TRI, provided you do not attempt to take either. Let us keep it that way.
I am sorry.
I have told Germany that I will attack Munich. I won't.
I am attacking Berlin instead.
Will you support me moving from Budapest to Rumania?
You do if you build them
I have no army in ALB—I believe you may be misinformed. My forces remain focused westward, where they belong. However, if your fleet moves closer to TRI or VEN, I will reassess my neutrality.
I want to help you fight Turkey.
Do you want me to help you against Italy?
They would be very useful if you want to attack Italy
I do not want conflict with you.
Yes, I'll help you attack Austria
I will not attack either TRI or VEN.
After I take BUD, I'll move into SER
I cannot afford to fight Italy right now.
But I thank you for the offer.
I might help you attack Munich in the fall.
Ok.
You are scared of the Ionian Sea.
I remain focused westward, as you have been assured. If your fleet moves toward TRI or VEN, do not expect me to remain neutral. Clarity is required, not empty promises.
I'm not fighting Italy right now.
England and Italy might attack me together.
Are you going to move into TYR and BOH?
No. I am going to defend Munich.
Austria is going to move from BUD to SER
No, sorry. I want to help you defend SER
What are you going to do?
I'm thinking England and Italy might attack me together.

No, I told him I was not going to attack him.

FRANCE: F ENG S A BUR - BEL
