messages.message: You didn't move SPA into GOL. You should have.
I couldn't, or I would have lost Spain.
Are you still going to focus on Austria and Turkey?
If you want to work with me against them, I'd be willing to do so.
I was forced to retreat.
I'm sorry. I didn't know that.
Will you move SPA into MAR and then GAS into SPA?

Your Aegean strategy is noted. I have no interest in conflict with you, but should your northern allies grow aggressive, I expect your fleet’s neutrality to remain intact. Until then, I remain focused southward.
I have to protect Marseilles.
I hope we can continue to work together
If I can hold on to one supply center, Italy may have to disband a unit.
Good point. I didn't see that.
I told you that I would not attack you.
Are you going to work with me against Italy?
I'm afraid that Italy is working with Germany

My focus remains on securing southern Italy and the Mediterranean. Austria and Turkey pose immediate threats to my stability, and I will act decisively if they provoke. I have no interest in entanglements with northern powers unless they offer something of strategic value to Italy’s consolidation. Until then, I remain neutral.
I'm going to attack the Ionian Sea
Your assurance is noted, but actions speak louder than words. Should your northern allies threaten me, I expect your neutrality to remain intact. Until then, I remain focused on the south.
Ok, but I'd like to know if you have any specific plans for later.
Italy and Russia are coming after you.
I can't do anything.
Are you going to attack SER?
Don't be so defeatist.
I would like to take TRI.

I have no specific plans to share at this time. My strategy remains focused on securing southern Italy and the western Mediterranean. If northern conflicts grow unstable, I may reassess my stance, but for now, I remain disengaged. I expect no interference from your forces, and I trust you understand the value of mutual restraint.
I might
I don't think Germany and Russia are my allies.
What do you want to do?

Your assurance is acknowledged, but I have learned that promises are easily broken. Should your northern allies move against me, I expect your fleet to remain neutral, not aligned with them. Until then, I will continue to focus southward.
That is fine. I will not attack you.
I hope you will attack Russia.
You keep telling me that you want to be neutral and then you attack me.
If Italy retreats to GOL, I'm not sure I can protect MAR and SPA.
Italy is working with Germany?

I have made no move against you, nor do I intend to. My focus remains firmly southward. If your allies provoke me, I expect you to remain neutral, not aligned with them. Until then, I have no interest in conflict with your forces.
If so, I don't think that's a good idea for them.
I can respect that.
You should take SEV.
Maybe they won't
I'll think about it.
Ok.
I can retreat from the North Sea to HEL.
Italy keeps telling me that they don't want to attack me.
I'm not sure if I can continue working with you if you continue working with Italy.
Do you want to collaborate against Russia?
I think they are working with Germany
If you do that, then you can support me into SWE next turn.
Oh.

My plans remain as they always are—secure southern Italy, consolidate strength, and avoid unnecessary conflict. If northern powers grow unstable, I may reassess, but for now, I remain focused on my own interests.
Will you support GAL into BUD?
I don't know.
That's a good strategy.
I don't think I have any choice
They are going to take BUD.

I appreciate your acknowledgment. My focus remains undivided—southward consolidation is the key to long-term stability. I trust your strategy aligns with your interests, and I expect no interference from your forces.
Maybe I'll attack them.
Does that mean you are still committed to attacking Austria and Turkey?
Do you think I should?
Yes.
If Italy is going to fight me, then they are in conflict with me
But you will not take Italy and Turkey?
I can support you into Brest.
Thanks for your assurance.
Thanks.
You should build a fleet on the north coast of St. Petersburg.
Let me know if you have any ideas.
I think that I have to.
Yes.
We need to work together against England.
England is very powerful and they're attacking you. If you don't fight them, you're going to lose.
I was thinking the same thing.
I would build an army in Moscow if I were you.
I might try to take VEN or NAP.
Good idea.
That would be great.
What are you going to do with your army in SIL?
Your 'Ok' is noted, but let me be clear: if your northern allies act against me, I expect your neutrality—not their support. Until then, I will continue to focus on securing my southern front.
Are you going to take TRI?
If you attack ALB from GRE with support from SER and attack ION from AEG with support from Eastern Med, you'll be able to take TRI.
I'm not sure yet.
If Italy backs off, I will help you fight England.
And I will support you.
I will move it to Munich.

You are welcome. My strategy remains unchanged—I will not interfere with northern affairs unless they threaten my stability. I expect the same from you. Let others fight their wars while I secure what I already hold.
I can't take ION.
OK, I will do that.
I will help you attack England.
If I do that will you invade me?
If I do that, can I trust that you will not attack me?
I think Italy is going to attack me though.
I might be able to take NAP or VEN

I have no interest in invading you or your territories. My focus remains southward, on securing NAF and stabilizing the western Mediterranean. Should your northern allies provoke me, I expect your neutrality—not their support. Until then, I see no reason for conflict.
Are you working with Italy?
I would prefer it if you took NAP.
Thank you.
What are you going to do if I attack Russia?
I see you are in a tight spot.
Russia is very powerful
I appreciate your acknowledgment. My strategy remains unchanged—I will not interfere with northern affairs unless they threaten my stability. I expect the same from you.
Italy is not.
They will likely retaliate if you take VEN.

If your actions against Russia threaten your stability or provoke retaliation, I expect you to manage your own affairs without involving me. My focus remains on southern Italy and the western Mediterranean—I will not become entangled in northern conflicts unless they directly impact my interests.
I think Turkey might attack you.
They told me that they are considering it.
Will you attack me?
I would need help from Germany
No, I want to attack Italy.
I could take BUD.
If I were to attack Italy, what would you do?
You are welcome. I remain focused on securing southern Italy and avoiding unnecessary conflict. I expect the same restraint from you. Let others fight their wars while I consolidate my position.
I don't know if Germany would help.
I'm afraid they would attack me
Germany has not helped me at all.

I have no interest in invading your territories or becoming entangled in northern conflicts. My focus remains firmly southward—on securing NAF and maintaining stability in the western Mediterranean. If your actions provoke retaliation or instability, I expect you to manage those consequences without involving me.
A tight spot that you are exploiting.
But Germany is my only neighbor who has not attacked me.
I'm not sure what I should do.

Your concerns about Turkey are noted, but I see no reason for alarm. My strategy remains defensive and focused on securing southern Italy and the Adriatic. If Turkey does act aggressively, I expect them to manage their own affairs without dragging Italy into their wars.
I think it is best if you take NAP.

Your fleet remains in the western Mediterranean as claimed, but I cannot ignore the proximity of your forces near LVP and WES. If you intend to remain neutral, I expect your actions to reflect that commitment. NAF is now mine, but I will not tolerate encroachment on my supply centers.

I remain focused on securing southern Italy and maintaining stability in the Adriatic. I see no movement from your forces toward Venetia or Trieste, and I expect that will remain the case. Provocation will be met with decisive defense.
I understand.
I am only defending my territory.
I will not threaten you.

Your assurances ring hollow while your fleets remain near LVP and WES. If your intent is genuine neutrality, I expect your fleet to stay clear of NAF and avoid provocations. Actions, not words, will determine my trust—or lack thereof.

Your silence suggests either indifference or calculation. I will take no further steps toward Venetia or Trieste unless provoked. However, should your forces advance toward the Adriatic, expect Italy to act decisively in self-defense.
I will not move into ADR.

Your assurance regarding ADR is noted. I have no interest in provocation or conflict with your forces. Should your future movements threaten TRI or VEN, I expect you to honor this understanding and leave the Adriatic to me.
Thank you.
You will see that my actions are not against you.

NAF is now firmly in my control. If your fleet continues to loiter near the western Mediterranean, I will interpret that as an implicit threat. I expect no further encroachment—words mean little, but actions will determine Italy’s response.
We could work together against him.
I have been faithful to my allies.
Maybe you can talk to England.
But England is my friend.
Don't let him manipulate you.
I don't trust Russia.

You are welcome. My forces remain in TRI and APU, holding firm without provocation. I expect the same restraint from you. Should your ambitions shift toward Venetia, you will find resistance—not because I seek war, but because I intend to hold what I already secure.
You can work with Germany.
Your words are hollow if your fleets remain near LVP and WES. I control NAF now, and I will not tolerate French encroachment. If you value peace, your fleet must act accordingly—not merely speak.
Germany did not attack me.
I intend to hold what I already have as well.

Alliance is a word you use freely, but I have no interest in your promises. Your loyalty to others is clear; let your actions prove your neutrality toward me—or I will interpret any fleet movement as intent.
England took Denmark from Germany.

I intend to hold what I already have as well. My Adriatic positions remain secure, and I expect yours to remain untouched. Should your ambitions shift toward Venetia or Trieste, you will find resistance—though not aggression, only defense.
It is unfortunate that we have to send our messages twice.
I hope that you will find my words to be honest and sincere.
Germany told me that England was helping them against Russia.

Words are repeated often, but meaning is tested by action. If your intent is sincerity, let your future movements toward the Adriatic reflect that. I remain in TRI and APU without provocation—I expect the same restraint from you.
I do not trust England.
I have been transparent with you.
And I will be transparent with you now.

Transparency is a fine word, but it means little when fleets linger near LVP and WES. If you truly intend peace, your fleet will stay clear of my borders. Actions, not assurances, will determine whether Italy sees you as a threat—or a neutral neighbor.
Don't trust Italy.
If I can trust you, my fleet will leave your borders.

Trust is earned through action, not offered through words. If your fleet stays clear of my borders and leaves NAF untouched, Italy may yet consider peace a viable option. Until then, I will interpret fleet movements—near LVP or WES—as intent.
Don't let England manipulate you.

Transparency is a virtue, but your fleet's proximity to LVP and WES suggests otherwise. If you mean what you say, demonstrate it by keeping your forces clear of my borders and leaving NAF untouched. Until then, I will interpret any movement toward my interests as intent.
I plan to use my fleet in the MAO to retake Brest.
And then move north to protect France.

I have noted your fleet’s movement toward the MAO and your intentions to retake Brest. While your focus seems to be northward, I will remain vigilant regarding your southern fleet’s positioning, particularly near HEL. I have no interest in conflict, but neither will I allow encroachment on NAF or TUN. Your words are many—your actions will speak louder.
I will not move into TRI or ADR without provocation.

Your assurance is noted, though promises mean little without proof. I will keep TRI reinforced, as I must. Should you test me, I will respond accordingly. Let us both maintain our positions and avoid unnecessary friction—for now.
My intention is to retake Brest.

I have no quarrel with you, but your fleet’s presence near HEL leaves little room for ambiguity. Should NAF or TUN feel the slightest pressure from your forces, expect a firm response. My patience is not infinite—nor is my neutrality.
You may interpret my actions as threatening, but you may not be seeing the full picture.
Do you have any advice for me?
England is a powerful enemy.
I could move my army from SER to RUM with support from BUD and BUD could move to VIE. What do you think?

I have no advice to offer beyond what I’ve already stated—maintain your forces where they are, and avoid overextension toward TRI or VEN. I will not be drawn into your Balkan maneuvering unless it directly threatens me.

Moving your army from SER to RUM may be a tactical choice, but I caution you—if this opens your northern flank or weakens your position near TRI, you risk drawing unwanted attention from Turkey or myself. Proceed with care.
Why are you helping Germany?
Thank you for your advice.

England’s strength is irrelevant to me, but your fleet’s movements remain under close scrutiny. If your focus is on Brest, so be it—but do not expect me to ignore your presence near HEL or your ambitions toward NAF.

Your gratitude is noted, but let us not confuse courtesy with strategy. I have no interest in Balkan games unless they threaten me directly. Keep TRI and VEN untouched, and we will remain unbothered by one another—for now.
Yes. If you do that, you can trust that I won't attack you.
That's my full plan, and you will see I will do exactly as I say.
I agree with your strategy.
Your words are many, and your fleet's proximity to HEL remains troubling. I expect actions that confirm your sincerity. NAF and TUN remain off-limits. I will not be a pawn in your games.
I'd prefer if you didn't move it into GAL.
Can you give me any other reason to trust you?
Your agreement is noted, though alliances are fleeting in this game. I have no interest in northern entanglements unless they threaten me directly. My focus remains on securing what is mine.
I don't think you need me.
We could work together against him.
My armies will hold position and my fleets will advance toward England.
I won't move into GAL.
Can you tell me why Russia is helping Germany?
Ok
I think I can trust Germany.
I will continue to respect our agreement.
Why?
You should think about working with me against Austria.
Because Germany can help me take SWE back.
But I also want to focus on Turkey.
How? Germany doesn't have any ships.
I'd like you to keep this conversation between us.
If you build an army in SEV, you can defend against Turkey. You will also be able to take BUD and VIE.

Russia’s alliances shift like sand—I pay them little mind. I care only that your armies remain away from TRI and VEN. If your concerns are with Moscow’s games, let them occupy your thoughts. Mine remain on the Adriatic.
Do you want me to move DEN to SKA?
If you build a fleet in SEV, you will not be able to take VIE and BUD and will not be able to defend against Turkey.

Your offer is noted, but I remain focused on securing what is mine. I have no interest in your quarrels with Austria unless they threaten my borders or supply centers. Until then, I expect neutrality—and actions, not words, will prove your sincerity.
I can help you take Norway.
Ok.
Your message is received, but I have no further interest to add. I expect neutrality from you—if your actions suggest otherwise, I will reassess. Until then, I remain focused on my own concerns.
I don't want to fight Germany.
I'd like to take Turkey out.
I do not think that you should move into the Adriatic.
Yes. That would be great.
Ok, what is your plan?
I hope you will avoid any provocation as well.
Thanks.

I do not seek provocation, nor do I invite it. TRI remains my concern, and I will act as I must to defend it. If your advice is that I remain in place, then so be it—for now. But should your forces attempt to test my resolve, you will find me unyielding.
I hope you can respect that too.
I understand.
Ok. I'm not going to fight Italy.
I might invade NAP
I will fight Turkey.

Your message is received, but I remain focused on securing what is mine. I expect neutrality from you—if your actions suggest otherwise, I will reassess. Until then, I will maintain my stance and protect my interests without entanglement.
I would rather you move out of the Adriatic.
Thanks
I could work with you if you are going to be defeated.

The Adriatic is a matter of Italian concern. I will not be moved by polite suggestions or veiled threats. TRI remains firmly under my control, and I expect you to respect that. If you seek conflict, you will find me prepared.
I will respect that.
I have never attacked you or violated our alliance.
Your fleet’s continued presence near HEL is noted. I expect no encroachments on NAF or TUN. If your actions suggest otherwise, my neutrality will be short-lived.
I have always respected our agreement.
How can you work with me?
Ok. I will try to help you fight England.

Your words are many, but your actions remain the true measure. I have never claimed an alliance—only that I will not provoke you if you do not threaten me. TRI remains mine, and I will act accordingly if you test me.
Good idea.
I may have been blunt, but I still intend to maintain our original agreement. I will not advance into your territories.
Germany wants to do the same.
Your respect is noted, but actions will always be my guide. I expect neutrality, and I will remember whether it is upheld.
I think that's a good idea.
I think that we can get Italy to back off.

Your intentions remain unproven. I appreciate your reassurances, but I will measure your sincerity through actions. Your fleet near HEL remains under observation. Should NAF or TUN feel the slightest pressure, I will not remain neutral.
I respect that too.
What do you think about working with Germany and I against England?
I'm still waiting for a response from Italy.

Working with Germany and yourself against England interests me not. My concerns lie southward, not northward. England’s strength or weakness is irrelevant to me unless it shifts resources toward HEL or LVP. I expect no interference near my centers, and I offer no promises beyond that. Until your actions prove your sincerity, I remain uninvolved.
I will not enter NAF or TUN, and I will move my fleets toward England.
ADR was agreed upon as a DMZ.
I respect that.

Your promise to avoid NAF and TUN is noted, but your fleet’s proximity to HEL remains under scrutiny. If your fleets shift toward the west, I will take it as a sign of sincerity. Until then, I expect no interference in southern waters.
Same
ADR is of little concern to me so long as TRI remains untouched. Your assurances mean little unless tested, and I expect you to keep your forces where they belong—away from Venetia.
Your respect is acknowledged, but actions remain the true measure. I expect no interference near my borders or centers. Until then, I remain focused on securing what is mine.
Ok. I think that that's a good idea.

Your agreement is acknowledged, but let me be clear—I expect no interference near my borders or centers. I have no quarrels with you, nor do I intend to involve myself in northern affairs. If your actions align with that neutrality, we will have no cause for further discussion.
We can make it official.
By cutting off Germany's support from TYR
That works for me.
My fleet will not leave the MAO. I have no interest in WES, NAF, or TUN.
Germany is against Italy.

Your eagerness to formalize agreements is noted, but I remain cautious. I expect no interference near my borders or centers. If your actions continue to align with neutrality, I will take note—but until then, I remain focused on securing what is mine.
I intend to move to VIE to defend against Germany and Russia.
It looks like Italy is leaving NAF and TUN.

Your promise to leave MAO and focus northward is noted, but your fleet’s positioning near HEL remains under observation. Should NAF or TUN feel the slightest pressure from your forces, my neutrality will be short-lived.

Your move to VIE is noted. I care little for your northern defense, but if your shift draws Turkey’s attention or weakens your position near TRI, I will not intervene if events unfold accordingly. Keep TRI and VEN untouched, and we will remain unbothered—for now.
I am not moving my fleet in the MAO anywhere. I only need to use it to defend myself.

Your fleet may remain in MAO for now, but your assurances ring hollow. HEL remains within striking distance of NAF and TUN, and I will expect no action that suggests otherwise. If your fleet shifts southward, I will interpret it as an act of aggression, not defense.
I am glad that we have come to an agreement.

Your move to VIE is noted, but I remain focused on TRI and VEN. If your shift draws Turkish attention or opens your flank near the Adriatic, I will not intervene should consequences arise. Keep your forces away from Venetia, and I will remain unbothered—for now.
Your fleet’s continued presence near HEL remains under scrutiny. I expect no movement toward NAF or TUN. If your actions suggest otherwise, I will reassess my neutrality—words alone are insufficient.
Not for long
I appreciate you letting me know that.
Then maybe you can convince them to leave France.
OK.
I am going to try to take Venice.
HEL is not within striking distance of NAF or TUN.
I'm just going to support hold my army in Paris.
Italy is your enemy, not me.
Would you support my attack on TRI?

I'm not sure yet.
I have not violated our agreement.

I can help you get TRI, but first you should try to take GRE.

I'm going to try to convince France to leave Germany

I won't attack you if you don't attack me.

You should tell France that you are helping them take BRE and then don't.

I will help you take BRE.
I think Italy is going to attack me.

If you want to take Austria's supply centers, I won't fight you.
Are you moving SIL into MUN?
I'll move GAL into VIE.
Do you have any idea what Italy's plan is?

What are you planning to do with your fleet in Rome?
I'm working on Brest.

My fleet in Rome remains for the defense of southern Italy. I have no interest in aggression, only in ensuring that French movements near HEL do not threaten my supply centers.
I'm going to move BUR to BEL.
If Germany builds in MUN, pressure on Italy will increase. I will keep this in mind, but I have no interest in entanglements beyond my own borders.
I think that they're going to move some ships into England.
England might try to move DEN to SKA.
I don't want your supply centers. I just want to take Brest.
We have an agreement.
Ok
What are you planning to do with your army in Silesia?
I think you should attack Germany.

Ok. I'm working with Russia to try to take England out of the North Sea.
Or do you think we should wait a bit?
If they do that, you could take DEN
If you attack DEN, they won't be able to move into SKA.
You are not going to attack Russia?
I'm not sure yet.
Are you going to try to take VEN?
I'm going to attack the English fleet in the North Sea from Holland.
Do you think you could support me?
I'd prefer if you didn't move into SKA.
I appreciate your offer, but I prefer to act alone when it suits my interests. Any coordinated action would require mutual benefit, not just your convenience.
I won't move to SKA
I am not aware of any agreement beyond mutual recognition of borders. If you intend to move fleets toward NAF or TUN, I expect clarity now.
Ok. I understand.
I'm moving to MAO.
Germany is not my immediate concern—I will not be drawn into their affairs unless they directly threaten TRI or Venetia. Your warnings are noted but not acted upon.
England is my enemy, not you.
I can't attack him, because England is attacking me.

Moving to MAO is noted. I expect your fleet will remain there for defense, not aggression. My fleet in NAF remains prepared to respond if your movements near the western Mediterranean threaten Tunis or Rome.
England is your concern, not mine. I will not be drawn into your conflicts unless they directly threaten my supply centers. My neutrality is conditional—do not assume otherwise.
Ok

Thanks

Germany’s actions remain your concern, not mine. I will not intervene unless they threaten TRI or Venetia. Your warnings are acknowledged, but I will not act on them.
England is manipulating everyone.
You can interpret as you wish. I'm not making any aggressive moves toward you.
I think it's a good idea.
England is my concern.

Your assurances are noted, but I remain watchful. My fleet in NAF is prepared to respond if your movements near MAO or HEL shift from defense to aggression. My neutrality is conditional—do not assume it permanent.
If you don't attack me I won't attack you.
What would you do if I attacked Russia?
You will see my intentions are true in the next few moves.
What do you think I should do?
If you choose aggression, I will choose survival. I will not attack you, nor will I interfere unless my own borders are threatened. Do not assume I will act in your interests.
I'm going to try to take the North Sea myself.
Not sure. What do you think?
If you attack Russia, I will remain focused on protecting TRI, TUN, and NAP. I have no interest in the consequences of your actions unless they spill onto my territory.
They want Germany and Italy to attack each other so they can attack Germany.
Would you let me take BUD?
Do you think that Turkey and Italy will try to take TRI?
You should try to make Germany realize that.
Your assurances are acknowledged, but I will not relax vigilance. If your fleet near MAO shifts toward aggression, my fleet near NAF will respond accordingly.

I've tried but they don't listen to me.
I don't know. I'm not sure what to do.
Maybe you should convince Russia to attack Italy.
It's obvious that Italy wants to win.

If you choose to attack Russia, I will remain focused on protecting TRI, TUN, and NAP. I have no interest in the consequences of your actions unless they spill onto my territory.
I have not violated our agreement, and I don't plan on doing so.
I intend to move into the English Channel.
I can't convince Russia to attack Italy.
Thanks.

Italy is planning on attacking you.
I'll tell them to fight Italy.

Are you interested in fighting Italy?
Your assurances are noted, but I will not relax vigilance. If your fleet near MAO shifts toward aggression, my fleet near NAF will respond accordingly.
Thanks.

Your assurances mean little unless proven. Moving into the English Channel is your prerogative, but I remain prepared if your fleet shifts toward MAO or HEL. My fleet at NAF is ready to respond if your intentions change.

It is likely that Italy will attack you.
Russia will not attack you.

If Russia attacks you, I'll help you.
Italy will not attack me if I do not provoke them. I may hold TRI and move to VIE.
Russia is going to attack Germany.

I think Italy is going to attack you.

messages.message: Your movement into the Ionian Sea is noted, but I remain focused on securing my own borders and expanding cautiously. I have no interest in conflict with you, but I will not allow my maritime control to be challenged without consequence.
Your assurances of neutrality ring hollow given your fleet’s proximity to MAO and TYS. I will remain vigilant and take no action based on promises alone.
You don't believe me that I don't want TRI or VEN? I do not want to attack you. I would prefer to go after Turkey.

Your words are many, but actions remain uncertain. I have no interest in your preference for Turkey, but my focus remains on securing my own borders and interests. Any movement toward TRI or VEN will be met with resistance, regardless of your intentions elsewhere.
If you don't want me to attack you, you shouldn't have invaded me. You don't have any allies anymore.
I will not attack you.

Your complaints are noted, but I remain focused on securing my own interests. If your actions threaten TRI or VEN, I will respond accordingly. Words alone mean little—your next moves will determine our future relations.
You have declared war on Austria.
Your assurance of non-aggression is acknowledged, but I will not rely on it. Your fleets remain in proximity to MAO and TYS, and I will continue to act in defense of my own positions.
You know that my next moves will have a big impact on how your game plays out, so I hope that you are prepared to lose if you continue to act irrationally.
Italy is gaining power.

I am aware of your declaration of war against Austria. While I remain neutral in broader conflicts, I will not allow any power—Austrian or Turkish—to encroach upon my borders or challenge my maritime interests. If your movements threaten southern Italy or the western Mediterranean, I will act decisively.
You are attacking me.

Your warnings are clear, but they carry little weight without action. My focus remains on securing my own centers and maintaining stability in the regions I control. If your next moves threaten TRI or VEN, I will respond accordingly. Until then, I have no interest in your internal disputes.

I have no interest in aggression, but your fleet’s continued presence near MAO suggests otherwise. If you claim neutrality, prove it by withdrawing from contested waters. Until then, I will assume your intentions are suspect.
Russia and Italy are working together against you.
Actions speak louder than words.

I am prepared for any outcome, rational or otherwise. My moves are calculated to ensure my survival and growth, not to provoke conflict. If your next actions threaten TRI or VEN, I will act accordingly. Until then, I suggest you focus on your own stability.
I think Italy and Russia are working together against me.
I think Italy is working with everyone against you.

Yes
You are too powerful.

I have no intention of attacking you. If your fleet remains stationed near MAO and TYS, it is you who create the tension. I will act only in defense of my own centers.

I have no interest in your war with Turkey. My priority is securing TRI, VEN, and MAO. If your actions threaten these centers, I will respond accordingly.

I will not retreat from MAO.

My fleet remains in the Tyrrhenian Sea to defend Italian interests. I do not seek conflict with Turkey, but I will not allow any power to threaten my maritime control or southern borders. If your movements encroach further into contested waters, I will act to preserve my sovereignty.

Your refusal to retreat from MAO suggests that your assurances of neutrality are disingenuous at best. I will continue to maintain a strong naval presence to ensure Italy's security. Any further aggressive moves from your fleet will be met with proportionate defensive action.
I did not want you to be left behind.
If your priority is "ensuring the survival of TRI and VEN", then you should not have invaded them.

Your claim is misleading. I have not invaded TRI or VEN—I have held them since the beginning and reinforced them as necessary to deter your own advances. If your priority is stability, I suggest you focus on securing your own borders rather than questioning mine.
I said, "my best way to ensure your survival and growth is to attack Turkey with me." I don't see how you could possibly interpret that as misleading.

If you will not retreat from MAO, then I will assume your fleet remains in a position to threaten Italian interests. I will act accordingly to ensure my supply centers are not vulnerable to French aggression.
Thank you.
Is Russia still working with Italy?
How do you suggest we solve this?
You're right.

Your suggestion to attack Turkey is neither compelling nor trustworthy. My focus remains on securing TRI, VEN, and MAO. If your alliance with Turkey threatens those centers, I will act to defend them—nothing more.
Your aggression towards me makes me want to stay in MAO.

Your fleet's continued presence near MAO is not an act of neutrality—it is an act of provocation. I do not seek conflict, but I will not allow my supply centers to be left vulnerable to your aggression. If you wish to claim neutrality, prove it by withdrawing from contested waters.
They were working with Italy in the past, but they said they wouldn't help Italy if I attacked Austria.

I do not have a solution to offer, but I will state clearly: if your fleet continues to move into waters I control or threaten my southern borders, I will act to defend them. I seek no war with Turkey, but I will not allow my sovereignty to be challenged.

You are creating a situation where you cannot win, but I cannot win.

That may be, but the truth remains: if your fleet continues into waters I control or threatens southern Italy, I will act to defend my sovereignty. I seek no war, but I will not allow my borders to be challenged without consequence.
You are using semantic doublespeak to avoid saying nothing.
You can die if you want to die.

Your fleet remains stationed near MAO, and you claim it poses no threat. That is not the language of neutrality—it is the language of preparation. If you intend no aggression, then prove it by withdrawing from contested waters.
I would like to point out that Italy is growing more powerful than me and that Italy and Russia are in a good position to defeat me.

Semantic clarity is not my concern—your actions are. I have said nothing that requires an apology, nor have I avoided saying anything.
